title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs project metadata, assets, pyinstaller 6.21, platforms, and clean launch from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for portable inspected game release candidate after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Asset Licenses
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 5029 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.

Inspect the Asset Licenses trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Asset Licenses.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Asset Licenses, keep Asset Licenses connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 5029 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. options.text: The Asset Licenses record says portable inspected game release candidate looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Asset Licenses.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Asset Licenses for portable inspected game release candidate.
The record names the assumption, applies Asset Licenses, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 4315 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.

content.title: Evidence defense
